rag_rules: Fix unresolved references in AGREE contracts by ensuring all identifiers are valid AADL features or declared helpers in the same annex.
Resolve type mismatches by aligning helper declarations with the types of their expressions, e.g., bool for comparisons, int for integers.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers in AGREE code that are not present in the model's configured dependencies.
generic_rules: When AGREE diagnostics report type errors, trace back to the first unresolved identifier or invalid helper on the same line and correct the root cause.
AGREE contracts must be placed in valid scopes: component types for assumptions/guarantees, implementations for bindings.
Use only visible AADL features or helpers declared within the same annex to avoid reference resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Constants and equations in AGREE must use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Component implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings like assignments.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce new package references or qualified names not present in the model's dependencies.
Do not add architecture elements to fix AGREE type errors; instead, resolve the root cause of the reference or type mismatch.
